steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: Before the blank: "...two other Black Americans who served in Congress" — a complete independent clause. After the blank: a list of two names. Independent clause + list = colon.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Rule: a colon after a complete independent clause introduces a list or explanation.

A — "Congress." A period ends the sentence, leaving "Ralph Harold Metcalfe of Illinois and George Henry White of North Carolina" as a sentence fragment with no verb. Fails.

B — "Congress:" A colon follows the complete clause "...who served in Congress" and correctly signals the list of names that follows. Correct.

C — "Congress;" A semicolon joins two independent clauses, but the name list that follows is not an independent clause. Fails.

D — "Congress" No punctuation runs the list of names directly into the clause with no signal, creating a confusing run-on. Fails.

B is correct.
